items.headline: High Wind Warning issued April 25 at 9:13PM AKDT until April 26 at 2:00AM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Southeast winds 35 to 55 mph with gusts up to 75 mph.
Visibility reduced to less than one half mile at times.

* WHERE...Pribilof Islands.

* WHEN...Until 2 AM AKDT Sunday.

* IMPACTS...High winds may move loose debris, damage property, and
cause power outages. Travel will be difficult.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Warmer air will slowly mix in from the south,
leading to mixed precipitation and a gradual changeover to rain.
items.instruction:
People are urged to secure loose objects that could be blown around
or damaged by the wind. Prepare for the possibility of widespread
power outages. Use caution if you must drive.

items.headline: Wind Advisory issued April 25 at 9:13PM AKDT until April 26 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
items.description:
* WHAT...South winds 35 to 45 mph, with gusts up to 60 to 75 mph
expected.

* WHERE...Unalaska and Nikolski.

* WHEN...Until 4 PM AKDT Sunday.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects and a few
power outages may result.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...The first of two rounds of strong southerly
winds will end later this afternoon, with a second round of
southerly winds arriving late tonight through Sunday morning. For
Unalaska, gusts of up to 65 mph are expected, with the strongest
wi
items.instruction:
People are urged to secure loose objects that could be blown around
or damaged by the wind. Use caution if traveling.

items.headline: High Wind Warning issued April 25 at 9:13PM AKDT until April 26 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Southwest winds 45 to 60 mph, with gusts up to 80 mph
expected.

* WHERE...Atka and Adak.

* WHEN...Until 4 PM AKDT Sunday.

* IMPACTS...High winds may move loose debris, damage property, and
cause power outages. Travel will be difficult.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Southerly winds gusting 35-45 mph will become
westerly this evening as the storm center moves through. Westerly
winds gust up to 70-80 mph  will continue through Sunday morning,
then slowly decrease through Sunday evening.
items.instruction:
People are urged to secure loose objects that could be blown around
or damaged by the wind. Prepare for the possibility of power outages.

items.headline: Special Weather Statement issued April 25 at 8:11AM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
items.description:
The Valdez Avalanche Center has issued a SPECIAL AVALANCHE STATEMENT
for the following areas: The Valdez and Thompson Pass area.


* WHAT...Dangerous avalanche conditions exist in the backcountry. Avalanche
danger is High at mid and upper elevations.


* WHERE...The Valdez and Thompson Pass area.


* WHEN...In effect from Saturday, 8:00 am to Sunday, 8:00am AKST.


* IMPACTS...There is a high chance that a person or machine could
trigger an avalanche today. Natural avalanches will also be
